stimulus: Mudskippers don't simply swim through the shallows. They can leap across the mud, traveling up to half a meter in a single bound. How ______blank A specialized tail flick combined with strong pectoral fins propels them across the wet surface.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: are they able to jump so far?
are they able to jump so far.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is end-of-sentence punctuation. The clause has the inverted word order of a direct question ("are they able to jump so far"), so it must end with a question mark.

Choice A is incorrect because the clause uses declarative word order rather than question word order, and an exclamation point doesn't match the neutral tone of the sentence. Choice C is incorrect because a declarative clause shouldn't begin with the question word "How" used this way and shouldn't have inverted-then-not word order. Choice D is incorrect because a period can't be used to punctuate a direct question.
